rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE M. HASBUN ET AL VS. 101 LOMBARD STREET CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two, From 101 Lombard Street Condominium Association, And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $12,337.50
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 31, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF MICHELLE HASBUN, MARY HASBUN Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two, From 101 Lombard Street Condominium Association, And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $12,337.50 is OFF CALENDAR, re noticed for department 301. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DICK MAK ET AL VS. ELDA AGAVE LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 31, 2022 line 2. NOTICE OF MOTION & MOTION TO STRIKE is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Motion is granted as to paragraphs 35-51, 60, 61, 65, 66 and 75. Plaintiff shall have 10 days to amend to explain the inconsistent position regarding the option taken in the UD action (verified complaint, opposition to defendant's motion for summary judgment) and this action, (i.e. whether option was properly exercised creating a binding agreement or not properly exercised rendering it null and void). Motion is otherwise denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MITRA AHANI VS. MOHAMMED RAHMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 31, 2022 line 4. PLAINTIFF MITRA AHANI Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant is GRANTED. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections to Form Interrogatories -Unlawful Detainer (Set One), Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One), Requests for Production of Documents and Tangible Things (Set One) to be received by Plaintiff no later than November 1, 2022 by 5:00 p.m. If defendant fails to provide complete timely responses as ordered, sanctions are awarded in the amount of $1,366.25 to be paid no later than November 30, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
